title: [FIRRTL] Fix port insertion/deletion involving domain info
body:
#8843 only mostly works for doing correct insertion of ports with domain info. There are known and unknown bugs that need to be fixed.

Consider:

``` firrtl
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit Domains:
  domain ClockDomain:

  public module Domains:
    input a : { b: String }
    input a_b: Domain of ClockDomain
    output b: UInt<5> domains [a_b]
    invalidate b
```

This fails after `LowerOpenAggs`:

```
Foo.fir:5:10: error: 'firrtl.module' op domain information for non-domain port 'b' must be an 'ArrayAttr'
  public module Domains:
         ^
Foo.fir:5:10: note: see current operation: 
"firrtl.module"() <{annotations = [], convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>, domainInfo = [[], @ClockDomain, 0 : ui32], layers = [], portAnnotations = [], portDirections = array<i1: false, false, true>, portLocations = [loc("Foo.fir":6:11), loc("Foo.fir":7:11), loc("Foo.fir":8:12)], portNames = ["a_b", "a_b", "b"], portSymbols = [], portTypes = [!firrtl.string, !firrtl.domain, !firrtl.uint<5>], sym_name = "Domains"}> ({
^bb0(%arg0: !firrtl.string, %arg1: !firrtl.domain, %arg2: !firrtl.uint<5>):
  %0 = "firrtl.invalidvalue"() : () -> !firrtl.uint<5>
  "firrtl.matchingconnect"(%arg2, %0) : (!firrtl.uint<5>, !firrtl.uint<5>) -> ()
}) : () -> ()
```

The domain info has a bare `IntegerAttr` when it should be an `ArrayAttr<IntegerAttr>` for the third port.

- h/t @dtzSiFive for finding this.
- h/t @uenoku for identifying other bugs here (that are not included in the issue).
